pattern: Every operation is just a cyclic rotation, and only two things matter to reach the target: how many of the n-1 rotation amounts actually land on t, and how many labeled sequences of k choices sum to a matching total shift -- the second part collapses to a two-state recurrence (back-to-start vs. any-other-shift) that a small matrix power evaluates for any k.
hints: Removing a suffix and moving it to the front is exactly a rotation of the whole string, so after k operations the string is s rotated by whatever the k chosen suffix lengths add up to, modulo the string length.
First figure out, among the n possible rotation amounts of s, how many of them actually produce t -- that count can be more than one if s repeats itself, or zero if t is not reachable at all.

Separately count, over exactly k operations each choosing one of the n-1 nonzero shift amounts, how many sequences sum to a shift of 0 versus how many sum to any one fixed nonzero shift -- by symmetry every nonzero target has the same count, so you only ever need to track those two numbers and combine them with the rotation count from the first step, all under the given modulus.

constraints.note: lowercase English letters only, same length as s

edge_inputs.description: smallest possible strings, the only nontrivial rotation reaches the target
smallest possible strings, one operation cannot return to the starting string
identical source and target with more than one operation available
every character is the same, so every rotation amount lands back on the string itself
a two-letter repeating pattern, so more than one rotation amount reaches the target even though source and target differ
target is not any rotation of the source at all, so the answer must be zero no matter how large k is
smallest strings with a larger operation count, forcing many repeated forced swaps
a longer string paired with a rotation of itself and a moderate operation count

stress.description: short strings over a tiny alphabet so repeated rotations are common, target sometimes a genuine rotation and sometimes not
strings over the full lowercase alphabet with a larger operation count
stress.generator_python:
import string
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(2, max(2, min(size, 24)))
    s = ''.join(rng.choice(string.ascii_lowercase) for _ in range(n))
    if rng.random() < 0.5:
        r = rng.randint(0, n - 1)
        t = s[n - r:] + s[:n - r]
    else:
        t = ''.join(rng.choice(string.ascii_lowercase) for _ in range(n))
    k = rng.randint(1, 300)
    return [s, t, k]
